san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings through multiple platforms including cable TV, online streaming, and phone call-in options. Live translations were available in over 50 languages to help non-English speakers follow along with city business.
keyTopics: Multiple viewing options available (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations offered in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible to residents who cannot attend in person
No public comment period during this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council cancelled the zoning hearing that was scheduled for March 25, 2026. Residents who had business to discuss at that hearing will need to wait until the next one, which is now set for April 8, 2026.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Next hearing rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that funds housing and community development projects. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to help residents with housing and support services.
keyTopics: Federal grants available for housing and community programs
Review of funding applications from local organizations
Approval of previous meeting records

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in the city. They covered several initiatives including a program to help businesses that may not have proper licenses get into compliance, summer promotional campaigns to boost local shopping, and financial incentives to attract and keep retail stores and startups in Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses become compliant without penalties
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - promoting local businesses to residents
Public-Private Partnerships for city events and sponsorships
Economic incentives to attract retail stores and startups to Cupertino
Business outreach efforts at shopping centers around the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle several delayed items and address two main issues: a plan to remove weeds growing around the city as part of a county-wide weed control program, and a discussion about how federal housing grant money will be spent in the coming year. Residents had the chance to comment on both the weed abatement program and the housing funding plans.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026 - removing overgrown weeds throughout Santa Clara
Federal HUD housing grant spending plan for 2026/27
Multiple items postponed or reconsidered from previous meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine business items. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency facility-sharing agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
